title: Of the Sons of Master and Man
venue: A. C. McClurg and Co., Chicago

license.note: Published 1903; public domain in the United States.

abstract: Of the Sons of Master and Man (1903), by W. E. B. Du Bois. From The Souls of Black Folk.
excerpt: Life treads on life, and heart on heart; We press too close in church and mart To keep a dream or grave apart. MRS. BROWNING. [Illustration] The world-old phenomenon of the contact of diverse races of men is to have new exemplification during the new...
